metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that docs/config.md contains (or the plan adds) a dedicated section on the session-log current-log fingerprint resolver, not just the session-ID precedence chain — or revise the cross-reference target to a file that actually covers it.
evidence: Plan text: 'keep the session-log **capture helper + auto-rotation** in `docs/user-guide.md`, cross-referencing `docs/config.md` for the fingerprint resolver'

docs/config.md line 451 is the ONLY mention of session_log + fingerprint: '`session_log` current-log fingerprint formerly put `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` before `REBAR_SESSION_ID`' — a one-sentence precedence-inversion historical note, not a resolver section
Grep of docs/config.md for 'fingerprint resolver', 'current-log fingerprint', 'session_log fingerprint': no matches
docs/config.md documents `resolve_session_id` (session ID resolver) in detail, but this is distinct from a session-log current-log fingerprint resolver
findings.finding: The plan asserts docs/config.md is the authoritative cross-reference 'for the fingerprint resolver' when keeping session-log auto-rotation docs in user-guide.md. A read of docs/config.md shows it documents the session-ID resolver (resolve_session_id precedence chain) but has no section titled or dedicated to a 'fingerprint resolver' for session_log rotation. The only mention of session_log + fingerprint in config.md is a single historical precedence-inversion note (line 451), not a documented resolver section. If this cross-reference destination doesn't exist, the plan's scope exclusion ('keep rotation in user-guide, fingerprint resolver in config.md') leaves the fingerprint resolver undocumented.
findings.location: What section / Design decisions paragraph: 'keep the session-log capture helper + auto-rotation in docs/user-guide.md, cross-referencing docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver'
scenarios: Implementer adds the cross-ref pointer 'see docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver' in user-guide.md, but a reader following it finds no such section — the pointer is a dead end
The session-log rotation fingerprint semantics remain effectively undocumented in any authoritative location after this story ships
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a 'Session-log current-log fingerprint' subsection to docs/config.md documenting how the fingerprint is computed and used for rotation, or (b) if that content belongs in docs/user-guide.md or docs/ticket-model.md, update the plan to add it there and drop the misleading cross-reference to docs/config.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether docs/config.md needs a new 'Session fingerprint resolver' section (and add it to scope + AC), or whether the docs/user-guide.md cross-reference should instead point to docs/session-id-shims.md where the env-var precedence chain is actually documented.
evidence: docs/config.md has no matches for 'session', 'REBAR_SESSION_ID', 'fingerprint', or 'resolver' (confirmed via search)
The fingerprint resolver description is in CLAUDE.md lines 228-242: 'taken from the shared session-id resolver (REBAR_SESSION_ID, then CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, then SESSION_ID; see docs/config.md)'
docs/session-id-shims.md covers the session-id env-var precedence chain
docs/config.md is listed in scope as an edit target but no AC or 'What' step adds the fingerprint resolver content there
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section instructs that docs/user-guide.md should cross-reference docs/config.md 'for the fingerprint resolver', but docs/config.md contains no content about the session fingerprint resolver, REBAR_SESSION_ID precedence chain, or auto-rotation logic. That content currently lives in CLAUDE.md (lines 228-242) and docs/session-id-shims.md. The cross-reference target is a missing/wrong edit target.
findings.location: What section: 'keep the session-log capture helper + auto-rotation in docs/user-guide.md, cross-referencing docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver'
scenarios: An implementer following the plan adds a cross-reference link from docs/user-guide.md to docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver, but readers following that link find nothing relevant there — a broken internal cross-reference.
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add a step to the 'What' section to add a 'Session fingerprint resolver' subsection to docs/config.md (covering REBAR_SESSION_ID → CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID → SESSION_ID precedence) and add a corresponding AC; or (b) change the cross-reference target in the 'What' description to docs/session-id-shims.md, which already covers this content.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the user-guide.md AC proving command to require content that does NOT already exist (e.g. `rg -n 'auto-rotation|fingerprint|current_session_log' docs/user-guide.md`) so the AC can only pass after the richer capture-helper + auto-rotation detail has been written there.
evidence: Proving command: `rg -n 'session-log append|append_session_log' docs/user-guide.md`
user-guide.md:170 already contains: `rebar session-log append "Spent the morning tracing the pager bug; see comment on <id>."`
CLAUDE.md 'Session logs' section contains rich auto-rotation / fingerprint / pointer semantics content not present in user-guide.md
Plan says: 'keep the session-log capture helper + auto-rotation in docs/user-guide.md'
findings.finding: The AC proving command for docs/user-guide.md (`rg -n 'session-log append|append_session_log' docs/user-guide.md`) matches a line that already exists today (user-guide.md:170 has `rebar session-log append …`), so the AC would pass vacuously without the implementer actually moving or expanding the auto-rotation and capture-helper detail from CLAUDE.md. The deliverable intent ('retain the session-log capture-helper section') is broader than what the proving command verifies, leaving the richer auto-rotation content (session fingerprint, pointer semantics, degradation) potentially unwritten.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'docs/user-guide.md retains the session-log capture-helper section'
scenarios: Implementer adds no new content to user-guide.md; proving command passes because the existing `session-log append` line matches; auto-rotation detail is never documented in docs/, only in CLAUDE.md — defeating the purpose of the story.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the proving command with: `rg -n 'auto-rotation|current_session_log|fingerprint' docs/user-guide.md` (≥ 1 match) — these phrases appear in CLAUDE.md but not in the current user-guide.md, so the command only goes green after the content is actually written.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC#5) to verify that `docs/user-guide.md` contains a cross-reference to `docs/config.md` for the fingerprint resolver (e.g. `rg -n 'config.md|fingerprint' docs/user-guide.md`).
evidence: 'keep the session-log capture helper + auto-rotation in docs/user-guide.md, cross-referencing docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver'
AC#5 only verifies: `rg -n 'session-log append|append_session_log' docs/user-guide.md` — no check for 'config.md' reference
ABSENCE: no AC contains 'config.md' or 'fingerprint'
findings.finding: The What section describes adding a cross-reference from docs/user-guide.md to docs/config.md ('cross-referencing docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver'), but no acceptance criterion verifies that this cross-reference was actually added.
findings.location: What section ('cross-referencing docs/config.md for the fingerprint resolver') and AC#5
scenarios: Implementer adds the session-log section but omits the config.md cross-reference; AC#5 passes anyway; the described deliverable is silently dropped.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC#5's proving command to: `rg -n 'session-log append|append_session_log' docs/user-guide.md && rg -n 'config.md' docs/user-guide.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the post-promotion fate of the `session_log` CREATE-row entry in `docs/event-schema.md`: either (a) the row is condensed to a one-liner + pointer to the new section, or (b) the row is left as-is and the new section adds only the non-event-body conceptual context — so an executor has an unambiguous target state.
evidence: Plan: 'Add a `## The session_log ticket type` section to `docs/event-schema.md` (promote the existing CREATE-row note into a real section)'

event-schema.md line 46: the CREATE row contains ~8 distinct behavioral facts about session_log inline, covering gate-exemption, graph exclusion, STATUS refusal, link restrictions, Jira exclusion, and title guidance — all agent-facing decision rules

Plan: 'do not restate event bodies (drift risk)' — this anti-drift instruction is stated for ticket-model.md but not clarified for the CREATE row vs. new section relationship inside event-schema.md itself
findings.finding: The plan says to 'promote the existing CREATE-row note into a real section' in `docs/event-schema.md` for `session_log`, but gives no instruction about the fate of the source CREATE-row entry. The CREATE row (event-schema.md line 46) is a dense, multi-sentence inline spec covering gate-exemption, lifecycle-exemption, graph exclusion, STATUS refusal, link restrictions, Jira exclusion, and the title convention. Without specifying whether the row is shortened, left as-is, or partially replaced by a pointer to the new section, an executor will either produce duplicate content (drift risk the plan explicitly wants to avoid) or silently strip the row's forward-compat/reducer context.
findings.location: What section / docs/event-schema.md — session_log section promotion
scenarios: Executor promotes the full CREATE-row content into the new section and leaves the row intact — the same behavioral rules appear twice inside event-schema.md, creating the very drift risk the plan warns against.

Executor strips the CREATE row to a one-liner pointer to the new section, removing the HLC/replay context readers need to understand which event type seeds the session_log lifecycle — a structural loss not sanctioned by the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the single combined `rg -c` with four separate `rg -q` checks (one per required term/topic) so the proving command confirms each topic is individually present: e.g. `rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md`.
evidence: 'rg -c' returns the count of matching lines for the combined alternation, not a per-branch count

'documents all four concept topics (idea status, hierarchy/containment, linking relations+promotion, tags surface + --set-tags caveat)' — four distinct topics, but the proving command is a single combined alternation with a shared ≥4 threshold
findings.finding: AC1's proving command `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md` counts lines matching ANY of the four alternation branches, not distinct per-topic matches. A document with 4+ lines mentioning only 'idea' passes ≥4 while covering zero of the other three topics; conversely, a complete document where all four terms appear on fewer than 4 lines fails. The command does not individually confirm all four concept topics are present.
scenarios: An executor adds a doc with the word 'idea' appearing 4 times but never mentions parent_id, discovered_from, or set-tags — AC1 passes even though three topics are absent
A thorough doc covering all four topics in a tight intro paragraph returns rg -c = 1, which is < 4, causing a false failure
findings.suggested_fix: rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md

findings.finding: The proving command for docs/ticket-model.md uses `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags'`, which counts only total matching LINES across all four patterns combined — a file containing 'idea' 10 times with no mention of 'set-tags' or 'parent_id' would pass the count check.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria — first bullet, `Proving:` line
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the single `rg -c` with four chained `rg -q` calls (one per required topic), each returning non-zero on failure: `rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set.tags' docs/ticket-model.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Raise AC3's proving-command threshold to require all three terms individually, e.g.: `rg -q 'gate-exempt' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q 'plan-review' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q '\-\-force' docs/concurrency.md`.
evidence: What section: 'a cascaded parent claim runs the parent's own plan-review gate; epics/stories are not gate-exempt; --force propagates' — three distinct claims required
AC3 proving command: 'rg -n 'gate-exempt|plan-review|--force' docs/concurrency.md' with only '(≥ 1 match)' threshold
findings.finding: AC3's proving command `rg -n 'gate-exempt|plan-review|--force' docs/concurrency.md` requires only ≥1 match from a 3-term alternation, so adding a single mention of `--force` satisfies it even if the plan-review gate behavior and the gate-exempt status are never documented. The What section requires all three nuances to be folded in.
scenarios: An executor adds only `--force propagates` to concurrency.md — AC3 passes but the plan-review gate concept and gate-exempt caveat are not documented, leaving the stated gap partially unfilled
findings.suggested_fix: rg -q 'gate-exempt' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q 'plan-review' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q -- '--force' docs/concurrency.md

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: The revised Acceptance Criteria drop the semantic-completeness verification that the original ACs explicitly required ('reviewer confirms via side-by-side diff in the change description; no rule dropped'). The revised ACs use existence/grep proofs only, which do not guarantee that every rule and nuance from the five CLAUDE.md sections lands in the target docs — content can be partially migrated and all revised ACs still pass green.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria (all five bullets) vs. original AC bullet #2
findings.suggested_fix: Add back the original AC #2 verbatim, or a revised equivalent: '- [ ] Change description includes a section-by-section checklist confirming every rule and nuance from the five CLAUDE.md source sections is present in the target doc (reviewer sign-off required; not provable by grep alone).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the gate-interaction text folded into `docs/concurrency.md` I4a, add an explicit cross-reference to `docs/plan-review-gate.md` where the gate-exempt type set (`bug`, `session_log`) is authoritatively defined — so the decision rule and its vocabulary are reachable from one hop.
evidence: Plan: 'Fold CLAUDE.md's cascade gate-interaction paragraph (a cascaded parent claim runs the parent's own plan-review gate; epics/stories are not gate-exempt; `--force` propagates) into `docs/concurrency.md` I4a'
docs/plan-review-gate.md line 222: 'Separately, bugs and session_logs are exempt from the whole gate (a distinct exemption axis, not part of container/leaf scrutiny)'
docs/concurrency.md has zero current matches for 'gate', 'plan-review', or 'gate-exempt' — confirmed by search
The plan specifies no cross-reference from I4a to plan-review-gate.md where the exempt-type enum is defined
findings.finding: The plan moves the gate-interaction paragraph (which names the gate-exempt type set: 'only `bug`/`session_log` are [gate-exempt]') into `docs/concurrency.md` I4a, but provides no instruction to cross-reference `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, which is the authoritative home of that exemption enum. An agent reading I4a after this change will encounter the gate-exempt decision rule without being directed to its definition, creating an orphaned processing protocol co-location gap.
findings.location: What section / docs/concurrency.md I4a — gate-interaction fold
scenarios: An agent or contributor reads docs/concurrency.md I4a post-change, sees 'epics/stories are not gate-exempt' and wants to know what IS gate-exempt — there is no pointer to docs/plan-review-gate.md, leaving the type vocabulary unresolvable from I4a alone.
A future contributor adds a new gate-exempt type, updates plan-review-gate.md, but misses I4a because the two docs are not cross-linked — the concurrency doc silently goes stale.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the gate-interaction paragraph in I4a: 'See [docs/plan-review-gate.md](plan-review-gate.md) for the full gate-exempt type set and the attestation model.'

findings.finding: The revised AC for docs/event-schema.md and user-guide.md ('gains a session_log ticket type section; user-guide.md retains the capture helper') has no proving command, making it unverifiable without manual inspection that could be omitted.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria — third bullet (event-schema.md / user-guide.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to that AC's bullet: `Proving: rg -n '## The session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md && rg -qn 'session.log' docs/user-guide.md`

findings.finding: The revised AC for `docs/event-schema.md` ('gains a `## The session_log ticket type` section') has no proving command, unlike every other revised AC which includes a shell one-liner. An executor cannot mechanically verify this deliverable was done.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria — third bullet: 'docs/event-schema.md gains a `## The session_log ticket type` section; user-guide.md retains the capture helper.'
findings.suggested_fix: Proving: `rg -n 'session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md && rg -n 'append_session_log\|session-log append' docs/user-guide.md`

findings.finding: The revised AC for user-guide.md ('user-guide.md retains the capture helper') has no verification step (no grep/test command), unlike every other revised AC, making it unverifiable as written.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria, third bullet: 'docs/event-schema.md gains a ## The session_log ticket type section; user-guide.md retains the capture helper'
findings.suggested_fix: Append: 'Proving: `rg -n 'capture.helper\|auto-rotation\|session.log' docs/user-guide.md`' to that AC bullet.

findings.finding: The revised AC for event-schema.md ('gains a ## The session_log ticket type section') also has no verification command, unlike the ticket-model.md and concurrency.md revised ACs.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria, third bullet: 'docs/event-schema.md gains a ## The session_log ticket type section'
findings.suggested_fix: Append: 'Proving: `rg -n 'session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md`' to that AC bullet.

findings.finding: The revised AC for `docs/event-schema.md` ('gains a `## The session_log ticket type` section') has no machine-checkable proving command, unlike all other revised ACs which include a grep or test command. The AC is verifiable only by manual inspection, creating an inconsistency and a merge-time verification gap.
findings.location: Revised Acceptance Criteria, third bullet: 'docs/event-schema.md gains a `## The session_log ticket type` section'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the third revised AC: `Proving: rg -n 'session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md && rg -n 'capture helper\|auto-rotation' docs/user-guide.md`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
